# ADR-260053: Two-Column Auth + Demo Panel Layout for Sign-In Screen

- **Date**: 2026-06-10
- **Status**: Accepted
- **Supersedes**: —
- **Superseded by**: —

## Context

The crystord_platform marketing site uses a two-column layout for the sign-in experience: a tabbed
auth card on the left and a "Try Demo" feature card on the right. The web app's `SignInPage` was
originally a minimal inline-styled form with no visual hierarchy matching the brand.

The decision is to port the layout structure (not colors) from the platform into the web app, using
the web project's CSS custom property token system as the authoritative brand source.

## Decision

- `SignInPage` is restructured into a two-column grid using a dedicated CSS file
  (`sign-in-page.css`) to enable responsive breakpoints.
- Left column: a tabbed auth card (Sign In / Sign Up) with ARIA tablist pattern, FormField-style
  label+input pairs, and an "or sign in with" divider.
- Right column: a `DemoPanel` sub-component that renders the feature list, "Try Demo" CTA, and
  "No registration required" note. `DemoPanel` is purely presentational; all demo auth logic
  stays in `SignInPage`.
- Responsive: a `@media (max-width: 640px)` rule collapses the grid to a single column and moves
  the demo card border from left to top.
- All color values use `var(--color-*)` tokens from `tokens.css`. No hardcoded hex in `.tsx` or
  `.css` files.
- A `--white` value is referenced as `#ffffff` only in `sign-in-page.css` for the panel
  background, matching the existing `var(--color-card-bg)` token which maps to `#ffffff`.

## Amendment: Sign-In Page Always Renders in Light Mode (2026-06-11)

The sign-in / sign-up page is permanently pinned to the light-mode token set, regardless of the
user's active theme preference (`data-theme="dark"` or `prefers-color-scheme: dark`).

**Rationale**: The sign-in page is a brand entry surface. A consistent, light-first appearance
aligns with the landing-page guidance in `branding.md` ("Open White as the dominant background")
and avoids a jarring first impression when the app theme differs from the OS preference.

**Implementation**: `.sign-in-page` in `sign-in-page.css` re-declares the full light-mode token
set as local custom properties. Because CSS custom properties resolve at the nearest ancestor, this
overrides any `[data-theme="dark"]` or `prefers-color-scheme` values without touching `tokens.css`.
`color-scheme: light` is also set on the element to keep browser-native form controls (inputs,
buttons) in their light appearance.

## Consequences

- SignInPage gains a clear visual hierarchy matching the platform's entry experience.
- Behavior is unchanged: same GraphQL mutations, same demo/demo credentials, same error handling.
- All existing architecture tests for SignInPage continue to pass because the key source strings
  (`htmlFor=`, `<label`, `role="alert"`, `try {`, `Username or Email`, etc.) are preserved.
- New architecture tests enforce the two-column layout, ARIA tab pattern, and DemoPanel presence.
